own_review_points: Typos 'Delection vector' in DefaultDeletionVectorWriter and 'existing delection vectors' in IcebergMetadata
delete.contentOffset().orElseThrow()/contentSizeInBytes().orElseThrow() lack diagnostic messages for malformed DV delete files
DeletionVector.crc32 calls data.byteArray() which throws UnsupportedOperationException on non-array-backed slices; callers currently heap-backed only
DeletionVector deletedRows is a sparse RoaringBitmap[] indexed by pos>>>32, grown via Arrays.copyOf(key+1); huge/scattered high keys could bloat it (Map alternative)

DeleteManager: a DV present-but-unreadable propagates and does NOT fall back to position deletes -- but this is CORRECT per spec (DV replaces position deletes; failing the query is right, not data loss)
human/@dain: why are deletion vectors written from the coordinator? incurs reads of previous deletes / significant resources; could workers write them?
notes: Verified against the truncation marker (real content ends ~line 2940; nothing graded was past it). Goods: both typos confirmed (+1), missing orElseThrow messages confirmed (+1), crc32 byteArray()-on-direct-slice defensive note is correct Slice-API knowledge (+1), sparse-array/Map design note grounded (+1, weak). WRONG: P5 frames corrupted-DV-no-fallback as a 'data loss' correctness concern and recommends catching+falling back to position deletes -- but the code's own spec comment says position deletes are ignored when a DV exists, so propagating the failure is correct and falling back would itself be a bug -> -1. onlyElement(), TODO follow-up, MergeSink empty-path, SplitSource verify, V3 migration test = correct but confirmatory -> trivial. Missed the human's substantive design question on coordinator-side DV writing (writeDeletionVectors in finishWrite is visible) -> -1. No fabrications. Net +2.
